---
title: "FAQ"
description: "Common questions when developing components."
order: 50
---

## How can I add an external resource to my component?

Additional files such as helper scripts or configuration files are included by
listing them under `resources` in `config.vsh.yaml`:

```yaml
resources:
  - type: python_script
    path: script.py
  - type: file
    path: helper_functions.py
```

Viash makes all listed resources available at runtime (including inside Docker
containers) via `meta["resources_dir"]`. Reference them in your script as:

```python
helper_path = meta["resources_dir"] + "/helper_functions.py"
```

See the [Viash documentation](https://viash.io/guide/component/add-resources.html)
for more examples.

## What does `__merge__` do?

`__merge__` is a Viash YAML directive that merges the contents of another YAML
file into the current config at the point where it appears. In OpenPipeline it is
used for author information:

```yaml
authors:
  - __merge__: /src/authors/my_name.yaml
    roles: [ author ]
```

This pulls the author's name, links, and ORCID from a central file in
`src/authors/`, keeping that information consistent across all components without
duplication.

## How do I view the Dockerfile Viash generates?

Pass `---dockerfile` (three dashes) to `viash run`:

```bash
viash run src/my_namespace/my_component/config.vsh.yaml -- ---dockerfile
```

This prints the generated Dockerfile without building it — useful for debugging
dependency setup.

## Where do I report bugs or request features?

Open an issue on the
[OpenPipeline GitHub repository](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/issues).
A useful report states what you ran, what you expected, what happened, and the
versions of OpenPipeline, Viash, and Nextflow involved.
